raw_findings.summary: When the background `&` is not at end-of-string or end-of-line, the rewrite produces invalid bash syntax — no separator between `}` and the trailing content.
raw_findings.detail: The assembly is `result = prefix + "{ " + middle + "& }" + suffix` where `suffix = result[amp_pos + 1:]`. For input `A && B & C`, `suffix = " C"`, producing `A && { B & } C`. In bash, `}` requires a preceding `;`, `&`, or newline — `} C` with only a space is a syntax error. The test suite confirms this gap: every test case ends with `&` at end-of-string or `&` immediately before `\n`. There is no test like `"A && B & C"` (content after `&` on the same line).

raw_findings.summary: The scanner unconditionally resets chain state on newline, so `A &&\nB &` (chain operator at end-of-line, command on next line) is left unrewritten and still hits the subshell-wait leak.
raw_findings.detail:
Bash in non-interactive mode treats `&&` or `||` at end of a line as a line continuation — the next line is still part of the same compound command, not a new statement. The reset block `if ch == "
" and paren_depth == 0 and brace_depth == 0: last_chain_op_end = -1` fires regardless of whether a pending chain operator exists. For `A &&
B &`, the sequence is: `A` → see `&&` → `last_chain_op_end` set; see `
` → `last_chain_op_end` reset to -1; see `B`; see `&` → `last_chain_op_end == -1` → no rewrite recorded. The output is `A &&
B &` unchanged, which bash still parses as `(A && B) &` — the subshell-wait bug is not fixed. The test `test_newline_resets_chain_state` tests `"A && B
C &"` (newline comes after `B`, not after `&&`) and its comment "A && newline starts a new statement" describes a case that is NOT what is being tested. The actual multiline continuation case `"A &&
B &"` is absent from the test suite.

raw_findings.summary: Backtick command substitution `` `...` `` is not recognised, so `&&` inside backticks is exposed to the chain-operator detector and can trigger a false rewrite.
raw_findings.detail: The loop handles `'`, `"`, and `(...)` as delimited regions. Backtick is not in any special-character branch and falls through to `_read_shell_token`. Looking at the token reader call path, `_read_shell_token` uses a break set that does not include backtick, so it reads the backtick as a regular character and stops at the next metachar. A sequence like `` `A && B` & `` would expose `&&` to the chain detector and produce a malformed rewrite. `$()` is safe because paren depth tracking covers it; backtick has no equivalent tracking. The test class `TestQuotingAndParens` covers `''`, `""`, `$()`, and `\&`, but not backticks.

raw_findings.summary: No test for backtick command substitution containing `&&`.
raw_findings.detail: Directly paired with f-003 — the confirmed scanner gap has no regression coverage. A test like `` assert rewrite("`A && B` &") == "`A && B` &" `` would document the correct expectation (or expose the current incorrect behaviour).

Directly paired with f-002 — the absent test is the only gap that allows the newline-reset bug to exist undetected. A test `assert rewrite("A &&\nB &") == "A && \n{ B & }"` (or whatever the corrected output should be) would both document the expected behaviour and serve as a regression gate.

In bash, `}` requires a preceding `;`, `&`, or newline — `} C` with only a space is a syntax error. The test suite confirms this gap: every test case ends with `&` at end-of-string or `&` immediately before `\n`. There is no test like `"A && B & C"` (content after `&` on the same line).

Looking at the token reader call path, `_read_shell_token` uses a break set that does not include backtick, so it reads the backtick as a regular character and stops at the next metachar. A sequence like `` `A && B` & `` would expose `&&` to the chain detector and produce a malformed rewrite. `$()` is safe because paren depth tracking covers it; backtick has no equivalent tracking. The test class `TestQuotingAndParens` covers `''`, `""`, `$()`, and `\&`, but not backticks.

Bash in non-interactive mode treats `&&` or `||` at end of a line as a line continuation — the next line is still part of the same compound command, not a new statement. The reset block `if ch == "

reviewer.raw_analysis:
<!-- model: claude-sonnet-4.6 (context-isolated independent reviewer) | round: 3 | template: v0.3 -->

## Section 1: Findings

### f-001
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, final rewrite assembly (~line 387 in diff)
- **What**: When the background `&` is not at end-of-string or end-of-line, the rewrite produces invalid bash syntax — no separator between `}` and the trailing content.
- **Why**: The assembly is `result = prefix + "{ " + middle + "& }" + suffix` where `suffix = result[amp_pos + 1:]`. For input `A && B & C`, `suffix = " C"`, producing `A && { B & } C`. In bash, `}` requires a preceding `;`, `&`, or newline — `} C` with only a space is a syntax error. The test suite confirms this gap: every test case ends with `&` at end-of-string or `&` immediately before `\n`. There is no test like `"A && B & C"` (content after `&` on the same line).
- **Severity estimate**: MEDIUM
- **Category**: logic_error

### f-002
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, newline reset logic (~line 264 in diff)
- **What**: The scanner unconditionally resets chain state on newline, so `A &&\nB &` (chain operator at end-of-line, command on next line) is left unrewritten and still hits the subshell-wait leak.
- **Why**: Bash in non-interactive mode treats `&&` or `||` at end of a line as a line continuation — the next line is still part of the same compound command, not a new statement. The reset block `if ch == "\n" and paren_depth == 0 and brace_depth == 0: last_chain_op_end = -1` fires regardless of whether a pending chain operator exists. For `A &&\nB &`, the sequence is: `A` → see `&&` → `last_chain_op_end` set; see `\n` → `last_chain_op_end` reset to -1; see `B`; see `&` → `last_chain_op_end == -1` → no rewrite recorded. The output is `A &&\nB &` unchanged, which bash still parses as `(A && B) &` — the subshell-wait bug is not fixed. The test `test_newline_resets_chain_state` tests `"A && B\nC &"` (newline comes after `B`, not after `&&`) and its comment "A && newline starts a new statement" describes a case that is NOT what is being tested. The actual multiline continuation case `"A &&\nB &"` is absent from the test suite.
- **Severity estimate**: LOW
- **Category**: semantic_equivalence

### f-003
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, scanner character loop (no backtick handling)
- **What**: Backtick command substitution `` `...` `` is not recognised, so `&&` inside backticks is exposed to the chain-operator detector and can trigger a false rewrite.
- **Why**: The loop handles `'`, `"`, and `(...)` as delimited regions. Backtick is not in any special-character branch and falls through to `_read_shell_token`. Looking at the token reader call path, `_read_shell_token` uses a break set that does not include backtick, so it reads the backtick as a regular character and stops at the next metachar. A sequence like `` `A && B` & `` would expose `&&` to the chain detector and produce a malformed rewrite. `$()` is safe because paren depth tracking covers it; backtick has no equivalent tracking. The test class `TestQuotingAndParens` covers `''`, `""`, `$()`, and `\&`, but not backticks.
- **Severity estimate**: MEDIUM
- **Category**: logic_error

### f-004
- **Where**: `tests/tools/test_terminal_compound_background.py`, `TestQuotingAndParens`
- **What**: No test for backtick command substitution containing `&&`.
- **Why**: Directly paired with f-003 — the confirmed scanner gap has no regression coverage. A test like `` assert rewrite("`A && B` &") == "`A && B` &" `` would document the correct expectation (or expose the current incorrect behaviour).
- **Severity estimate**: LOW
- **Category**: missing_test

### f-005
- **Where**: `tests/tools/test_terminal_compound_background.py`
- **What**: No test for multiline continuation (`A &&\nB &`).
- **Why**: Directly paired with f-002 — the absent test is the only gap that allows the newline-reset bug to exist undetected. A test `assert rewrite("A &&\nB &") == "A && \n{ B & }"` (or whatever the corrected output should be) would both document the expected behaviour and serve as a regression gate.
- **Severity estimate**: LOW
- **Category**: missing_test

---

## Section 2: Observations

### o-001
- **Where**: `tools/environments/base.py`, `execute` method (~line 213 in diff)
- **What**: The import `from tools.terminal_tool import _rewrite_compound_background` is inside the method body, not at module top.
- **Why**: Python caches imports so there is no per-call overhead in practice. However, if the import fails (circular import, missing module), the failure surfaces at first execution call rather than at module load. Whether this matters depends on the import graph of `base.py` — not visible in the diff.

### o-002
- **Where**: `tools/environments/base.py`, call site
- **What**: `_rewrite_compound_background` expects `str`, but `exec_command` (returned by `_prepare_command`) might be a `list` in environments that use list-form commands.
- **Why**: The function does `n = len(command)` and iterates character by character — this would raise `TypeError` if `command` is a list. Whether `_prepare_command` always returns a string is not visible in the diff.

### o-003
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, `&>` redirect detection (~line 349)
- **What**: The `&>` redirect is detected by looking ahead one character (`command[i+1] == ">"`). If `&` is the last character in the string, this guard is correct (`i+1 < n`), but what about `& >` (space between)?
- **Why**: Bash allows `& >` with whitespace in some contexts. The detector's look-ahead is single-character and does not skip spaces before `>`. Whether this causes false positives or missed redirects depends on whether agents generate shell commands with `& >` spacing — not determinable from the diff alone.

### o-004
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, brace depth logic (~line 308)
- **What**: A `{` is only treated as a brace group opener if immediately followed by whitespace or newline (`command[i+1].isspace()`). The expression `{cmd && other &}` (no space after `{`) is valid bash for some shells but not treated as a brace group here.
- **Why**: POSIX requires whitespace after `{` in brace groups, so this check is correct for POSIX shell. Whether the target environment enforces strict POSIX is not determinable from the diff.

### o-005
- **Where**: `tools/terminal_tool.py`, `_rewrite_compound_background`, rewrite assembly
- **What**: Applying rewrites back-to-front preserves earlier indices, but the loop modifies `result` in-place across iterations. If two rewrite targets overlap (unlikely but theoretically possible with pathological input), later indices may shift.
- **Why**: Normal shell commands will not have overlapping rewrite targets at depth 0. This is a theoretical concern about the algorithm's correctness invariant rather than a practical risk.

---

## Section 3: Overall Assessment

The diff introduces a well-structured and defensively tested rewriter for the `A && B &` subshell-wait pattern. The test suite is comprehensive for the cases it covers, and the Findings/Observations split in this review reflects that most issues are edge cases rather than fundamental design flaws. Two substantive logic bugs exist: the `} suffix` syntax error (f-001, medium — affects any command with content after `&` on the same line) and the multiline continuation miss (f-002, low — affects `A &&\nB &` forms which bash parses as compound but the rewriter treats as two statements). A third bug exists with backtick substitution (f-003, medium), which the test suite does not cover. The import-inside-method pattern and the `exec_command` type assumption in `base.py` are minor hygiene items worth addressing but not blocking.
